steps.title: Apply similarity correspondence
steps.content: Triangle CAE ~ triangle CBD tells us the vertex correspondence: C↔C, A↔B, E↔D. In similar triangles, corresponding angles are equal. The angle at vertex A in the first triangle equals the angle at vertex B in the second: ∠CAE = ∠CBD.

steps.title: Determine ∠CAE
steps.content: Since ∠CAE = ∠CBD (from corresponding angles) and ∠CBD = 56° (given), we have ∠CAE = 56°. That matches choice B — B is correct.
